libraryDescription:
The tool.Graph3D API library, hereafter the Graph3D library, is a library for controlling 3D graph software.

The Graph3D library is an extension library and is not included in the standard libraries defined by the VCSSL language specification. Therefore, support for the Graph3D library implementation is not guaranteed in every VCSSL implementation.

The specification of the Graph3D library is defined assuming control of widely used general-purpose 3D graph software from VCSSL, and it is not intended for a specific 3D graph application. In an implementation of the Graph3D library, what kind of 3D graph software is adopted as the control target depends on the implementation, the vendor of the implementation, its version, or plugins that provide communication functions with graph software. Because of these circumstances, the functions provided by the Graph3D library should not be expected to behave in exactly the same way across different implementations.

Even in the same implementation, because the Graph3D library is not a standard library, its implementation is not guaranteed to behave permanently in the same way. Keep in mind that the implementation of the Graph3D library is only an extension feature supported independently by the implementation. For example, if the graph software receives a major update, the behavior of the Graph3D library may also change slightly as a result.

In the RINEARN VCSSL Runtime and VCSSL-supported software equipped with it, such as Rinearn Processor,the Graph3D library is supported, and its implementation adopts the graph software RINRARN Graph 3D for scientific and technical fields.Since the RINRARN Graph 3D program itself, RinearnGraph3D.jar, is bundled in the distribution package of the VCSSL Runtime,nothing needs to be prepared separately.

function0.name: newGraph3D
function0.description: Creates a 3D graph with the default size, assigns its unique identifier, the graph ID, and returns it.
function0.returnValue: (int type) The ID of the created graph.

function1.name: newGraph3D
function1.declaration: int newGraph3D( int x, int y, int width, int height, string title )
function1.description: Creates a 3D graph with the specified size, assigns its unique identifier, the graph ID, and returns it.
function1.parameters:
(int type) x : The X coordinate of the top-left corner of the graph window. 
(int type) y : The Y coordinate of the top-left corner of the graph window. 
(int type) width : The width of the graph window. 
(int type) height : The height of the graph window. 
(string type) title : The title of the graph window.
function1.returnValue: (int type) The ID of the created graph.

function2.name: getGraph3D
function2.description: If there is a special graph started and allocated by the implementation, gets its graph ID. Otherwise, creates a new one.
function2.returnValue: (int type) The obtained or created graph ID.

function3.name: deleteGraph3D
function3.parameters: (int type) graphID : The ID of the target graph.

function4.name: clearGraph3D
function4.description: Clears the contents of the graph.
function4.parameters: (int type) graphID : The ID of the target graph.

function5.name: getGraph3DGraphics
function5.description: Converts the drawing contents held by the graph into graphics resource compatible with the Graphics library.The converted graphics resource can be processed with a 2D or 3D renderer and so on.
function5.parameters: (int type) graphID : The ID of the target graph.

function6.name: setGraph3DLocation
function6.declaration: void setGraph3DLocation( int graphID, int x, int y )
function6.description: Sets the location of the graph window.
function6.parameters:
(int type) graphID : The ID of the target graph. 
(int type) x : The X coordinate of the top-left corner of the graph window. 
(int type) y : The Y coordinate of the top-left corner of the graph window.

function7.name: setGraph3DSize
function7.declaration: void setGraph3DSize( int graphID, int width, int height )
function7.description: Sets the size of the graph window.
function7.parameters:
(int type) graphID : The ID of the target graph. 
(int type) width : The width of the graph window. 
(int type) height : The height of the graph window.

function8.name: hideGraph3D
function8.parameters: (int type) graphID : The ID of the target graph.

function9.name: showGraph3D
function9.parameters: (int type) graphID : The ID of the target graph.

function10.name: setGraph3DFile
function10.declaration: void setGraph3DFile( int graphID, string filePath )
function10.description: Loads a file and plots it on the graph.
function10.parameters:
(int type) graphID : The ID of the target graph. 
(string type) filePath : The file name or path of the file to plot.

function11.name: setGraph3DFile
function11.declaration: void setGraph3DFile( int graphID, string filePath, string mode )
function11.description:
Loads a file in the specified mode and plots it on the graph.
Specify the mode as one of the following string values.
AUTO
A mode that automatically determines the format from the contents of the file
MATRIX_CSV
A mode that loads the file as a comma-separated matrix format
MATRIX_STSV
A mode that loads the file as a tab- or space-separated matrix format
3COL_CSV or 3COLUMNS_CSV
A mode that loads the file as a comma-separated three-column format
3COL_STSV or 3COLUMNS_STSV
A mode that loads the file as a tab- or space-separated three-column format
4COL_CSV or 4COLUMNS_CSV
A mode that loads the file as a comma-separated four-column format
4COL_STSV or 4COLUMNS_STSV
A mode that loads the file as a tab- or space-separated four-column format
Note that, regarding delimiters, the file may still be loaded even if the specified mode and the actual file contents do not match, for example, a CSV file in an STSV mode or vice versa.However, that behavior depends on the correction mechanism on the graph-software side, and there is no guarantee that it will still work in future versions. Therefore, specify the mode correctly whenever possible.
function11.parameters:
(int type) graphID : The ID of the target graph. 
(string type) filePath : The file name or path of the file to plot. 
(string type) mode : The mode of the file to plot.

function12.name: setGraph3DFile
function12.declaration: void setGraph3DFile( int graphID, string file[ ] )
function12.description: Loads multiple files and plots them on the graph.
function12.parameters:
(int type) graphID : The ID of the target graph. 
(string[] type) file : The file name or path of each file to plot.

function13.name: setGraph3DFile
function13.declaration: void setGraph3DFile( int graphID, string file[ ], string mode[ ] )
function13.description:
Loads multiple files in the specified modes and plots them on the graph.
Specify each mode as one of the following string values.
AUTO
A mode that automatically determines the format from the contents of the file
MATRIX_CSV
A mode that loads the file as a comma-separated matrix format
MATRIX_STSV
A mode that loads the file as a tab- or space-separated matrix format
3COL_CSV or 3COLUMNS_CSV
A mode that loads the file as a comma-separated three-column format
3COL_STSV or 3COLUMNS_STSV
A mode that loads the file as a tab- or space-separated three-column format
4COL_CSV or 4COLUMNS_CSV
A mode that loads the file as a comma-separated four-column format
4COL_STSV or 4COLUMNS_STSV
A mode that loads the file as a tab- or space-separated four-column format
Note that, regarding delimiters, the file may still be loaded even if the specified mode and the actual file contents do not match, for example, a CSV file in an STSV mode or vice versa.However, that behavior depends on the correction mechanism on the graph-software side, and there is no guarantee that it will still work in future versions. Therefore, specify the mode correctly whenever possible.
function13.parameters:
(int type) graphID : The ID of the target graph. 
(string[] type) file : The array storing the file names or paths of the files to plot. 
(string[] type) mode : The array storing the modes of the files to plot.

function14.name: setGraph3DData
function14.declaration: void setGraph3DData( int graphID, string data )
function14.description: Loads text written in the same format as the contents of a file and plots it on the graph.
function14.parameters:
(int type) graphID : The ID of the target graph. 
(string type) data : The text written in the same format as the contents of a file.

function15.name: setGraph3DData
function15.declaration: void setGraph3DData( int graphID, float dataX[ ], float dataY[ ], float dataZ[ ] )
function15.description: Loads float arrays storing coordinate values and plots them on the graph.
function15.parameters:
(int type) graphID : The ID of the target graph. 
(float[] type) dataX : The array storing the X coordinates, indexed by point index. 
(float[] type) dataY : The array storing the Y coordinates, indexed by point index. 
(float[] type) dataZ : The array storing the Z coordinates, indexed by point index.

function16.declaration: void setGraph3DData( int graphID, float dataX[ ][ ], float dataY[ ][ ], float dataZ[ ][ ] )

function16.parameters:
(int type) graphID : The ID of the target graph. 
(float[][] type) dataX : The array storing the X coordinates, indexed by Y-direction index on the mesh and X-direction index on the mesh. 
(float[][] type) dataY : The array storing the Y coordinates, indexed by Y-direction index on the mesh and X-direction index on the mesh. 
(float[][] type) dataZ : The array storing the Z coordinates, indexed by Y-direction index on the mesh and X-direction index on the mesh.

function17.name: setGraph3DData
function17.declaration: void setGraph3DData( int graphID, float dataX[ ][ ][ ], float dataY[ ][ ][ ], float dataZ[ ][ ][ ] )

function17.parameters:

(float[][][] type) dataY : The array storing the Y coordinates, indexed by series index, Y-direction index on the mesh, and X-direction index on the mesh. 
(float[][][] type) dataZ : The array storing the Z coordinates, indexed by series index, Y-direction index on the mesh, and X-direction index on the mesh.

(int type) graphID : The ID of the target graph. 
(float[] type) dataX : The array storing the X coordinates, indexed by point index. 
(float[] type) dataY : The array storing the Y coordinates, indexed by point index.

function21.name: setGraph3DConfigurationFile
function21.declaration: void setGraph3DConfigurationFile( int graphID, string file )

line : The contents of the configuration code.

function27.name: setGraph3DAutoRange
function27.declaration: void setGraph3DAutoRange( int graphID, bool autoX, bool autoY, bool autoZ )

function32.name: setGraph3DTick
function32.declaration: void setGraph3DTick( int graphID, double xTickCoords[ ], string xTickLabels[ ], double yTickCoords[ ], string yTickLabels[ ], double zTickCoords[ ], string zTickLabels[ ], double colorBarTickCoords[ ], string colorBarTickLabels[ ] )

(double[] type) zTickCoords : The coordinate values of the tick positions on the Z axis. 
(string[] type) zTickLabels : The labels of the tick marks on the Z axis. 
(double[] type) colorBarTickCoords : The coordinate values of the tick positions on the color bar. 
(string[] type) colorBarTickLabels : The labels of the tick marks on the color bar.

function37.name: setGraph3DCameraAngle
function37.declaration: void setGraph3DCameraAngle( int graphID, float horizontalAngle, float verticalAngle, string mode )
function37.description: Sets the camera angle.Specify the vector from the origin of the graph to the camera position, the camera-direction vector, by using the horizontal angle and the vertical angle based on the zenith axis described below for the horizontalAngle and verticalAngle arguments.The vertical angle specifies the angle between the camera-direction vector and the zenith axis.The horizontal angle specifies the direction of the projection of the camera-direction vector onto the plane perpendicular to the zenith axis.Here, the zenith axis can be selected from the X, Y, or Z axis by specifying one of the following values in the mode argument:<dl><dt>ZENITH_X</dt><dd>An angle-specification mode in which the X axis is treated as the zenith axis</dd><dt>ZENITH_Y</dt><dd>An angle-specification mode in which the Y axis is treated as the zenith axis</dd><dt>ZENITH_Z</dt><dd>An angle-specification mode in which the Z axis is treated as the zenith axis</dd></dl>
function37.parameters:
(int type) graphID : The ID of the target graph. 
(float type) horizontalAngle : The horizontal angle. 
(float type) verticalAngle : The vertical angle. 
(string type) mode : The angle-specification mode. See the list above for the available values.

function38.name: setGraph3DCameraMagnification
function38.declaration: void setGraph3DCameraMagnification( int graphID, float magnification )
function38.parameters:
(int type) graphID : The ID of the target graph. 
(float type) magnification : The camera magnification.

function39.name: setGraph3DCameraDistance
function39.declaration: void setGraph3DCameraDistance( int graphID, float distance )
function39.parameters:
(int type) graphID : The ID of the target graph. 
(float type) distance : The camera distance.

function40.name: setGraph3DCameraPerspective
function40.declaration: void setGraph3DCameraPerspective( int graphID, bool perspective )
function40.description: Sets whether the camera perspective is enabled.
function40.parameters:
(int type) graphID : The ID of the target graph. 
(bool type) perspective : True to enable perspective, or false to disable it.

function41.name: setGraph3DOption
function41.declaration: void setGraph3DOption( int graphID, string option, bool value )
function41.description: Sets an option item of the graph. The item names depend on the implementation. Representative option item names are as follows:<dl><dt>WITH_POINTS</dt><dd>Point plot</dd><dt>WITH_LINES</dt><dd>Line plot</dd><dt>WITH_MESHES</dt><dd>Mesh plot</dd><dt>WITH_SURFACES</dt><dd>Surface plot</dd><dt>WITH_MEMBRANES</dt><dd>Surface plot (Same as WITH_SURFACES)</dd><dt>FLAT</dt><dd>Flattening</dd><dt>FRAME</dt><dd>Frame drawing</dd><dt>SCALE</dt><dd>Scale drawing</dd><dt>GRID_LINE</dt><dd>Grid-line drawing</dd><dt>GRADATION</dt><dd>Gradient coloring by Z values</dd><dt>ALPHA</dt><dd>Translucent drawing</dd><dt>LOG_X</dt><dd>Display the X axis in logarithmic scale</dd><dt>LOG_Y</dt><dd>Display the Y axis in logarithmic scale</dd><dt>LOG_Z</dt><dd>Display the Z axis in logarithmic scale</dd><dt>REVERSE_X</dt><dd>Reverse the X axis</dd><dt>REVERSE_Y</dt><dd>Reverse the Y axis</dd><dt>REVERSE_Z</dt><dd>Reverse the Z axis</dd></dl>
function41.parameters:
(int type) graphID : The ID of the target graph. 
(string type) option : The name of the option item. 
(bool type) value : True to enable the option, or false to disable it.

function42.name: setGraph3DMenu
function42.declaration: void setGraph3DMenu( int graphID, string option, string value )
function42.description:
This function is effectively used only for label settings, and it can now be replaced by functions such as setGraph3DLabel and setGraph3DLabelX, Y, or Z.Because that is more readable, it is recommended to use that form from now on unless there is a specific reason not to.

Sets a menu item of the graph. The item names depend on the implementation. Representative menu item names are as follows:
SET_LABEL_X
The X-axis label
SET_LABEL_Y
The Y-axis label
SET_LABEL_Z
The Z-axis label
function42.parameters:
(int type) graphID : The ID of the target graph. 
(string type) option : The name of the menu item. 
(string type) value : The value to set for the menu item.

function43.name: setGraph3DAnimation
function43.declaration: void setGraph3DAnimation( int graphID, string option, bool state )
function43.description: Enables or disables graph animation. The available animation modes depend on the implementation. Representative animation modes are as follows:<dl><dt>TRACE</dt><dd>Animate points while leaving trajectories</dd><dt>INDEX</dt><dd>Animate points</dd><dt>INDEX-SERIES</dt><dd>Animate series</dd><dt>INDEX-X</dt><dd>Animate lines in the X direction of mesh data</dd><dt>INDEX-Y</dt><dd>Animate lines in the Y direction of mesh data</dd><dt>RANGE-X</dt><dd>Slide the plot range in the X direction</dd><dt>RANGE-Y</dt><dd>Slide the plot range in the Y direction</dd><dt>RANGE-Z</dt><dd>Slide the plot range in the Z direction</dd></dl>
function43.parameters:
(int type) graphID : The ID of the target graph. 
mode : The animation mode. 
(bool type) state : True to enable animation, or false to disable it.

function44.name: exportGraph3D
function44.declaration: void exportGraph3D( int graphID, string filename, string format )
function44.description: Outputs the graph as an image.
function44.parameters:
(int type) graphID : The ID of the target graph. 
(string type) filename : The output file name or path. 
(string type) format : The format, either "PNG" or "JPEG".

function45.name: exportGraph3D
function45.declaration: void exportGraph3D( int graphID, string filename, string format, float quality )
function45.description: Outputs the graph as an image.
function45.parameters:

(string type) filename : The output file name or path. 
(string type) format : The format, either "PNG" or "JPEG". 
(float type) quality : The quality, from 0.0 to 100.0.

function10.declaration: void setGraph3DFile( int graphID, string filePath )
function10.description: Loads a file and plots it on the graph.
function10.parameters:
(int type) graphID : The ID of the target graph. 
(string type) filePath : The file name or path of the file to plot.

function11.name: setGraph3DFile
function11.declaration: void setGraph3DFile( int graphID, string filePath, string mode )
function11.description:
Loads a file in the specified mode and plots it on the graph.
Specify the mode as one of the following string values.
AUTO
A mode that automatically determines the format from the contents of the file
MATRIX_CSV
A mode that loads the file as a comma-separated matrix format
MATRIX_STSV
A mode that loads the file as a tab- or space-separated matrix format
3COL_CSV or 3COLUMNS_CSV
A mode that loads the file as a comma-separated three-column format
3COL_STSV or 3COLUMNS_STSV
A mode that loads the file as a tab- or space-separated three-column format
4COL_CSV or 4COLUMNS_CSV
A mode that loads the file as a comma-separated four-column format
4COL_STSV or 4COLUMNS_STSV
A mode that loads the file as a tab- or space-separated four-column format
Note that, regarding delimiters, the file may still be loaded even if the specified mode and the actual file contents do not match, for example, a CSV file in an STSV mode or vice versa.However, that behavior depends on the correction mechanism on the graph-software side, and there is no guarantee that it will still work in future versions. Therefore, specify the mode correctly whenever possible.
function11.parameters:
(int type) graphID : The ID of the target graph. 
(string type) filePath : The file name or path of the file to plot. 
(string type) mode : The mode of the file to plot.

function12.name: setGraph3DFile
function12.declaration: void setGraph3DFile( int graphID, string file[ ] )
function12.description: Loads multiple files and plots them on the graph.
function12.parameters:
(int type) graphID : The ID of the target graph. 
(string[] type) file : The file name or path of each file to plot.

function13.name: setGraph3DFile
function13.declaration: void setGraph3DFile( int graphID, string file[ ], string mode[ ] )
function13.description:
Loads multiple files in the specified modes and plots them on the graph.
Specify each mode as one of the following string values.
AUTO
A mode that automatically determines the format from the contents of the file
MATRIX_CSV
A mode that loads the file as a comma-separated matrix format
MATRIX_STSV
A mode that loads the file as a tab- or space-separated matrix format
3COL_CSV or 3COLUMNS_CSV
A mode that loads the file as a comma-separated three-column format
3COL_STSV or 3COLUMNS_STSV
A mode that loads the file as a tab- or space-separated three-column format
4COL_CSV or 4COLUMNS_CSV
A mode that loads the file as a comma-separated four-column format
4COL_STSV or 4COLUMNS_STSV
A mode that loads the file as a tab- or space-separated four-column format
Note that, regarding delimiters, the file may still be loaded even if the specified mode and the actual file contents do not match, for example, a CSV file in an STSV mode or vice versa.However, that behavior depends on the correction mechanism on the graph-software side, and there is no guarantee that it will still work in future versions. Therefore, specify the mode correctly whenever possible.
function13.parameters:
(int type) graphID : The ID of the target graph. 
(string[] type) file : The array storing the file names or paths of the files to plot. 
(string[] type) mode : The array storing the modes of the files to plot.

function37.name: setGraph3DCameraAngle
function37.declaration: void setGraph3DCameraAngle( int graphID, float horizontalAngle, float verticalAngle, string mode )
function37.description: Sets the camera angle.Specify the vector from the origin of the graph to the camera position, the camera-direction vector, by using the horizontal angle and the vertical angle based on the zenith axis described below for the horizontalAngle and verticalAngle arguments.The vertical angle specifies the angle between the camera-direction vector and the zenith axis.The horizontal angle specifies the direction of the projection of the camera-direction vector onto the plane perpendicular to the zenith axis.Here, the zenith axis can be selected from the X, Y, or Z axis by specifying one of the following values in the mode argument:<dl><dt>ZENITH_X</dt><dd>An angle-specification mode in which the X axis is treated as the zenith axis</dd><dt>ZENITH_Y</dt><dd>An angle-specification mode in which the Y axis is treated as the zenith axis</dd><dt>ZENITH_Z</dt><dd>An angle-specification mode in which the Z axis is treated as the zenith axis</dd></dl>
function37.parameters:
(int type) graphID : The ID of the target graph. 
(float type) horizontalAngle : The horizontal angle. 
(float type) verticalAngle : The vertical angle. 
(string type) mode : The angle-specification mode. See the list above for the available values.

function38.declaration: void setGraph3DCameraMagnification( int graphID, float magnification )
function38.parameters:
(int type) graphID : The ID of the target graph.

function39.name: setGraph3DCameraDistance
function39.declaration: void setGraph3DCameraDistance( int graphID, float distance )
function39.parameters:

function40.declaration: void setGraph3DCameraPerspective( int graphID, bool perspective )
function40.description: Sets whether the camera perspective is enabled.
function40.parameters:

function41.name: setGraph3DOption
function41.declaration: void setGraph3DOption( int graphID, string option, bool value )
function41.description: Sets an option item of the graph. The item names depend on the implementation. Representative option item names are as follows:<dl><dt>WITH_POINTS</dt><dd>Point plot</dd><dt>WITH_LINES</dt><dd>Line plot</dd><dt>WITH_MESHES</dt><dd>Mesh plot</dd><dt>WITH_SURFACES</dt><dd>Surface plot</dd><dt>WITH_MEMBRANES</dt><dd>Surface plot (Same as WITH_SURFACES)</dd><dt>FLAT</dt><dd>Flattening</dd><dt>FRAME</dt><dd>Frame drawing</dd><dt>SCALE</dt><dd>Scale drawing</dd><dt>GRID_LINE</dt><dd>Grid-line drawing</dd><dt>GRADATION</dt><dd>Gradient coloring by Z values</dd><dt>ALPHA</dt><dd>Translucent drawing</dd><dt>LOG_X</dt><dd>Display the X axis in logarithmic scale</dd><dt>LOG_Y</dt><dd>Display the Y axis in logarithmic scale</dd><dt>LOG_Z</dt><dd>Display the Z axis in logarithmic scale</dd><dt>REVERSE_X</dt><dd>Reverse the X axis</dd><dt>REVERSE_Y</dt><dd>Reverse the Y axis</dd><dt>REVERSE_Z</dt><dd>Reverse the Z axis</dd></dl>
function41.parameters:
(int type) graphID : The ID of the target graph. 
(string type) option : The name of the option item. 
(bool type) value : True to enable the option, or false to disable it.

function42.name: setGraph3DMenu
function42.declaration: void setGraph3DMenu( int graphID, string option, string value )
function42.description:
This function is effectively used only for label settings, and it can now be replaced by functions such as setGraph3DLabel and setGraph3DLabelX, Y, or Z.Because that is more readable, it is recommended to use that form from now on unless there is a specific reason not to.

Sets a menu item of the graph. The item names depend on the implementation. Representative menu item names are as follows:
SET_LABEL_X
The X-axis label
SET_LABEL_Y
The Y-axis label
SET_LABEL_Z
The Z-axis label
function42.parameters:
(int type) graphID : The ID of the target graph. 
(string type) option : The name of the menu item. 
(string type) value : The value to set for the menu item.

function43.name: setGraph3DAnimation
function43.declaration: void setGraph3DAnimation( int graphID, string option, bool state )
function43.description: Enables or disables graph animation. The available animation modes depend on the implementation. Representative animation modes are as follows:<dl><dt>TRACE</dt><dd>Animate points while leaving trajectories</dd><dt>INDEX</dt><dd>Animate points</dd><dt>INDEX-SERIES</dt><dd>Animate series</dd><dt>INDEX-X</dt><dd>Animate lines in the X direction of mesh data</dd><dt>INDEX-Y</dt><dd>Animate lines in the Y direction of mesh data</dd><dt>RANGE-X</dt><dd>Slide the plot range in the X direction</dd><dt>RANGE-Y</dt><dd>Slide the plot range in the Y direction</dd><dt>RANGE-Z</dt><dd>Slide the plot range in the Z direction</dd></dl>
function43.parameters:
(int type) graphID : The ID of the target graph. 
mode : The animation mode. 
(bool type) state : True to enable animation, or false to disable it.

function44.parameters:

(string type) format : The format, either "PNG" or "JPEG".

function45.name: exportGraph3D
function45.declaration: void exportGraph3D( int graphID, string filename, string format, float quality )
function45.description: Outputs the graph as an image.
function45.parameters:

(string type) filename : The output file name or path. 
(string type) format : The format, either "PNG" or "JPEG". 
(float type) quality : The quality, from 0.0 to 100.0.
